en.name: Misal Pav
en.description: A heritage morning favorite with sprouted moth beans and a fiery tarri, topped with crunchy farsan.
en.chefNotes: The secret to a great Misal is the 'Kat' (thin spicy gravy). Use plenty of oil to ensure the 'Tarri' floats on top — it's the soul of the dish. For an authentic Kolhapuri experience, add a pinch of jaggery to balance the heat.

instructions.title: Preparing the Sprouts
instructions.text: Pressure cook the sprouted moth beans with turmeric and salt until soft but not mushy. Drain excess water and set aside.

instructions.title: The Masala Base
instructions.text: Heat oil in a deep pan. Add mustard seeds, cumin, and curry leaves. Sauté onions, ginger-garlic paste until golden brown.

instructions.title: Building the Kat
instructions.text: Add the Misal Masala, red chili powder, and goda masala. Stir in the cooked sprouts and the drained water (kat) for a thin gravy.

instructions.title: Simmering the Rassa
instructions.text: Simmer for 10-12 minutes until a layer of red oil (tarri) forms on top. Adjust salt and spice as per preference.

instructions.title: Assembling
instructions.text: To serve, ladle the spicy curry into a bowl, top generously with farsan, raw onions, and coriander. Serve hot with buttered Pav.

mr.name: मिसळ पाव
mr.description: अंकुरलेल्या मटकीपासून बनवलेली तिखट मिसळ, त्यावर फरसाण, कांदा आणि कोथिंबीर घालून गरमागरम पावासोबत दिली जाते.
mr.chefNotes: खरी कोल्हापुरी चव मिळवण्यासाठी तिखट तर्री तयार करा आणि सर्व्ह करताना लिंबू द्यायला विसरू नका.

instructions.title: मटकी शिजवा
instructions.text: अंकुरलेली मटकी हळद आणि मीठ घालून कुकरमध्ये मऊ होईपर्यंत शिजवा.

instructions.title: मसाला तयार करा
instructions.text: तेलात मोहरी, जिरे, कढीपत्ता टाका. कांदा, आलं-लसूण पेस्ट सोनेरी होईपर्यंत परता.

instructions.title: कट तयार करा
instructions.text: मिसळ मसाला, लाल तिखट आणि गोडा मसाला घाला. शिजलेली मटकी आणि पाणी घालून पातळ रस्सा करा.

instructions.title: रस्सा उकळवा
instructions.text: १०-१२ मिनिटे उकळवा, तर्री तरंगू लागेपर्यंत. चवीनुसार मीठ आणि तिखट समायोजित करा.

instructions.title: सर्व्ह करा
instructions.text: वाटीत मिसळ घाला, वरून फरसाण, कांदा आणि कोथिंबीर घाला. लोणी लावलेल्या पावासोबत सर्व्ह करा.

en.name: Vada Pav
en.description: Mumbai's iconic street burger — crispy spiced potato fritter nestled in a soft pav with fiery chutneys.
en.chefNotes: The key to perfect Vada Pav is the dry garlic chutney — roast garlic with dry coconut and red chilies until deeply fragrant. This is what makes Mumbai's version legendary.

instructions.title: Prepare the Filling
instructions.text: Make a paste of green chilies, ginger, and garlic. Heat oil, add mustard seeds and curry leaves.

instructions.title: Season the Potatoes
instructions.text: Add turmeric, mashed potatoes, and salt. Mix well and let cool. Shape into round balls.

instructions.title: Make the Batter
instructions.text: Make a batter with besan, baking soda, salt, and water. It should be thick enough to coat.

instructions.title: Deep Fry
instructions.text: Dip each potato ball in the batter and deep fry until golden and crispy.

instructions.title: Assemble & Serve
instructions.text: Slice pav, apply dry garlic chutney and green chutney. Place the vada inside and press gently. Serve with fried green chili.

mr.name: वडा पाव
mr.description: मुंबईचा प्रसिद्ध स्ट्रीट बर्गर — कुरकुरीत बटाट्याचा वडा मऊ पावात, चटण्यांसोबत.
mr.chefNotes: खरी चव लसूण चटणीत आहे — लसूण, सुके खोबरे आणि लाल मिरच्या भाजून बारीक वाटा.

instructions.title: सारण तयार करा
instructions.text: हिरव्या मिरच्या, आलं आणि लसूण वाटा. तेलात मोहरी आणि कढीपत्ता फोडणी द्या.

instructions.title: बटाटे मसालेदार करा
instructions.text: हळद, मॅश केलेले बटाटे आणि मीठ घाला. चांगले मिसळा आणि गोळे तयार करा.

instructions.title: पीठ तयार करा
instructions.text: बेसन, बेकिंग सोडा, मीठ आणि पाणी मिसळून घट्ट पीठ तयार करा.

instructions.title: तळा
instructions.text: प्रत्येक बटाट्याचा गोळा पिठात बुडवून सोनेरी होईपर्यंत तळा.

instructions.title: सर्व्ह करा
instructions.text: पाव कापा, लसूण चटणी आणि हिरवी चटणी लावा. वडा ठेवा आणि दाबा. तळलेल्या मिरचीसोबत सर्व्ह करा.

en.name: Puran Poli
en.description: A festive sweet flatbread stuffed with spiced chana dal and jaggery — a Ganesh Chaturthi essential.
en.chefNotes: The secret to thin, even Puran Poli is to roll gently from the center outward. If the filling leaks, your dough portion is too small. Let the puran (filling) cool completely before stuffing.

instructions.title: Cook the Dal
instructions.text: Cook chana dal until very soft. Drain excess water completely.

instructions.title: Make the Puran
instructions.text: Add grated jaggery to hot dal and cook until jaggery melts and mixture thickens. Add cardamom and nutmeg powder. Mash until smooth.

instructions.title: Prepare the Dough
instructions.text: Prepare dough with wheat flour, all-purpose flour, oil, turmeric, salt, and water. Rest for 30 mins.

instructions.title: Stuff & Roll
instructions.text: Flatten a dough ball, place puran in center, seal and roll out gently into thin roti.

instructions.title: Cook on Tawa
instructions.text: Cook on a hot tawa with ghee on both sides until golden spots appear. Serve warm with generous ghee on top.

mr.name: पुरणपोळी
mr.description: गोड चणाडाळीचं पुरण भरलेली मऊ पोळी, तुपासोबत गरमागरम खाल्ली जाते.
mr.chefNotes: पुरण अगदी बारीक वाटा आणि पोळी लाटताना मैद्याचा वापर करा म्हणजे मऊ होतात.

instructions.title: डाळ शिजवा
instructions.text: चणाडाळ मऊ होईपर्यंत शिजवा आणि पाणी काढून टाका.

instructions.title: पुरण तयार करा
instructions.text: शिजवलेली डाळ गूळ आणि वेलची पूड घालून परतवा. थंड करून बारीक वाटा.

instructions.title: पीठ मळा
instructions.text: गव्हाचे पीठ, मैदा, हळद, मीठ आणि तेल मिसळून मऊ पीठ मळा.

instructions.title: पोळी लाटा
instructions.text: पिठाचा गोळा करा, पुरण भरा आणि हळूवारपणे लाटा.

instructions.title: भाजा
instructions.text: तव्यावर दोन्ही बाजूंनी भाजा. तुपासोबत गरमागरम सर्व्ह करा.

en.name: Ukadiche Modak
en.description: Sacred steamed dumplings filled with coconut and jaggery — Lord Ganesha's favorite offering.
en.chefNotes: Keep the rice dough covered at all times — it dries out quickly and cracks. If the dough cracks while shaping, add a drop of hot water and knead again.

instructions.title: Prepare the Filling
instructions.text: Dry roast poppy seeds. Mix coconut and jaggery on low heat until jaggery melts. Add cardamom and poppy seeds. Cook until thick.

instructions.title: Make the Dough
instructions.text: Boil water with oil and salt. Add rice flour, mix quickly, cover and let it steam for 5 mins. Knead until smooth.

instructions.title: Shape the Modaks
instructions.text: Flatten dough into thin disc. Place filling in center. Bring edges together, pleat and shape into a modak.

instructions.title: Steam to Perfection
instructions.text: Steam the modaks for 10-15 minutes until the outer shell turns translucent. Serve warm with a drizzle of ghee.

en.name: Thalipeeth
en.description: A rustic multi-grain flatbread patted by hand on a hot tawa — wholesome, earthy, and protein-packed.
en.chefNotes: The bhajani flour mix is the star — it's traditionally a custom blend of roasted wheat, rice, jowar, bajra, and spices. Each family has their own secret ratio.

instructions.title: Mix the Dough
instructions.text: Mix bhajani flour with chopped onion, coriander, green chili, cumin, turmeric, and salt. Add water and knead into soft dough.

instructions.title: Shape on Tawa
instructions.text: Wet your palms. Pat dough directly on a greased hot tawa into a flat disc. Make a small hole in center.

instructions.title: Cook Until Crispy
instructions.text: Pour oil around the edges and in the hole. Cook on medium heat until golden and crispy on both sides.

instructions.title: Serve Hot
instructions.text: Serve hot with white butter and yogurt.

en.name: Sabudana Khichdi
en.description: Pearly sago pearls tossed with roasted peanuts, cumin, and curry leaves — a beloved fasting delicacy.
en.chefNotes: The soaking is everything. Under-soaked sabudana will be hard; over-soaked will be mushy. The pearls should be soft to touch but not falling apart.
ingredients: Salt to taste (rock salt for fasting)

instructions.title: Soak the Sabudana
instructions.text: Wash sabudana thoroughly and soak for 6-8 hours. Drain completely — pearls should be swollen and separate.

instructions.title: Temper the Spices
instructions.text: Heat oil in a pan. Add cumin seeds and curry leaves. Let them splutter.

instructions.title: Cook the Potatoes
instructions.text: Add cubed potatoes and cook until golden and tender.

instructions.title: Combine & Cook
instructions.text: Add green chilies, soaked sabudana, crushed peanuts, sugar, and salt. Mix gently. Cook covered on low heat for 5-7 minutes.

instructions.title: Finish & Serve
instructions.text: The sabudana should turn translucent when done. Add lemon juice, garnish with coriander, and serve hot.

mr.name: साबुदाणा खिचडी
mr.description: भिजवलेले साबुदाणे, शेंगदाणे आणि बटाट्यापासून बनवलेली उपवासाची चमचमीत खिचडी.
mr.chefNotes: साबुदाणे रात्रभर भिजवा आणि शिजवताना हलक्या हाताने हलवा, चिकट होणार नाहीत.

instructions.title: साबुदाणे भिजवा
instructions.text: साबुदाणे ४-५ तास पाण्यात भिजवा आणि पाणी काढून टाका.

instructions.title: शेंगदाणे तयार करा
instructions.text: शेंगदाणे भाजून जाडसर कुटा.

instructions.title: खिचडी शिजवा
instructions.text: तेलात जिरे, मिरच्या आणि कढीपत्ता फोडणी द्या. बटाटे शिजवा.

instructions.title: साबुदाणे घाला
instructions.text: भिजवलेले साबुदाणे आणि शेंगदाणे घाला. पारदर्शक होईपर्यंत शिजवा.

instructions.title: सर्व्ह करा
instructions.text: लिंबाचा रस आणि कोथिंबीर घालून गरमागरम सर्व्ह करा.

en.name: Bharli Vangi
en.description: Stuffed baby eggplants simmered in a rich coconut-peanut masala — a wedding feast classic.
en.chefNotes: Use small, tender purple brinjals for best results. The masala should be slightly coarse, not fine powder. Jaggery and tamarind create the signature sweet-sour balance.

instructions.title: Prepare the Brinjals
instructions.text: Slit the small brinjals into quarters keeping the stem intact.

instructions.title: Make the Stuffing
instructions.text: Dry roast peanuts and sesame seeds. Grind coarsely with coconut. Mix with all spice powders, jaggery, tamarind, and salt.

instructions.title: Stuff the Brinjals
instructions.text: Stuff the masala mixture generously into each slit brinjal.

instructions.title: Slow Cook
instructions.text: Heat oil, arrange stuffed brinjals. Sprinkle remaining masala and water. Cover and cook on low heat for 20-25 minutes until tender.

en.name: Kothimbir Vadi
en.description: Crispy coriander fritters steamed then pan-fried — an addictive teatime companion.
en.chefNotes: The batter consistency is crucial — too thin and they won't hold shape, too thick and they'll be dense. Add just enough water to make a spreadable paste.
ingredients: 2 cups fresh coriander leaves, finely chopped

instructions.title: Mix the Batter
instructions.text: Mix besan, rice flour, coriander, sesame seeds, cumin, turmeric, chili powder, sugar, salt, and oil.

instructions.title: Steam the Mixture
instructions.text: Add water to make thick batter. Spread on greased plate, steam for 15-20 minutes.

instructions.title: Cut into Shapes
instructions.text: Let cool slightly, then cut into diamond or square shapes.

instructions.title: Pan Fry
instructions.text: Shallow fry the pieces until crispy and golden on both sides. Serve hot with green chutney.

instructions.title: Pan Fry
instructions.text: Shallow fry the pieces until crispy and golden on both sides. Serve hot with green chutney.

en.name: Zunka Bhakar
en.description: A rustic farmer's meal of spiced gram flour crumble served with hearty millet flatbread.
en.chefNotes: Stir continuously while adding besan to avoid lumps. The zunka is done when it turns golden and separates from the pan.
ingredients: Jowar or bajra bhakar for serving

instructions.title: Temper the Spices
instructions.text: Heat oil, add mustard seeds and cumin seeds. Let them crackle. Add curry leaves, green chilies, and garlic.

instructions.title: Sauté the Onions
instructions.text: Add chopped onions and cook until translucent. Add turmeric and salt.

instructions.title: Cook the Besan
instructions.text: Reduce heat to low. Add besan gradually, stirring continuously. Cook until crumbly and dry.

instructions.title: Serve Rustic
instructions.text: Garnish with coriander. Serve hot with bhakar (millet flatbread) and raw onion.

instructions.title: Sauté the Onions

instructions.title: Cook the Besan

instructions.title: Serve Rustic
instructions.text: Garnish with coriander. Serve hot with bhakar (millet flatbread) and raw onion.

en.name: Chole Bhature
en.description: Spiced chickpea curry paired with puffy fried bread — North India's most iconic brunch indulgence.
en.chefNotes: The tea bag trick gives the chole their signature dark color. For restaurant-style richness, add a tablespoon of anardana (pomegranate seeds) powder to the gravy.

instructions.title: Cook the Chickpeas
instructions.text: Pressure cook soaked chickpeas with salt, tea bag, and water for 5-6 whistles until soft.

instructions.title: Make the Bhature Dough
instructions.text: Mix flour, yogurt, baking powder, salt, and oil. Knead into soft dough. Rest for 2 hours.

instructions.title: Cook the Masala
instructions.text: Heat oil, add cumin seeds, ginger, garlic. Sauté onions until golden. Add tomato puree and spices.

instructions.title: Simmer the Chole
instructions.text: Add cooked chickpeas with cooking liquid. Simmer for 20 minutes, mashing some for thickness.

instructions.title: Fry the Bhature
instructions.text: Roll out dough into oval shapes. Deep fry until puffed and golden. Serve with chole, pickled onions, and chutney.

mr.name: छोले भटुरे
mr.description: मसालेदार छोल्यांसोबत फुगलेले भटुरे — पंजाबी नाश्त्याचा राजा.
mr.chefNotes: छोले रात्रभर भिजवा आणि अमचूर पावडर शेवटी घाला.

instructions.title: चणे भिजवा
instructions.text: काबुली चणे रात्रभर भिजवून मऊ होईपर्यंत शिजवा.

instructions.title: छोले बनवा
instructions.text: कांदा-टोमॅटो मसाला परतवून चणे घाला.

instructions.title: भटुरे पीठ मळा
instructions.text: मैदा, दही, मीठ आणि तेल मिसळून पीठ मळा.

instructions.title: भटुरे तळा
instructions.text: पीठ लाटून गरम तेलात फुगवून तळा.

instructions.title: सर्व्ह करा
instructions.text: गरमागरम छोले भटुऱ्यांसोबत कांदा-लिंबू लावून सर्व्ह करा.

en.name: Dal Makhani
en.description: Velvety black lentils slow-simmered overnight with butter and cream — the crown jewel of North Indian cuisine.
en.chefNotes: The secret is patience. True Dal Makhani should simmer for at least 4-6 hours on the lowest possible flame. The slow cooking transforms ordinary lentils into silky luxury.
ingredients: 1 cup whole black urad dal, soaked overnight

instructions.title: Pressure Cook the Dal
instructions.text: Pressure cook soaked urad dal and rajma together with salt until very soft (8-10 whistles).

instructions.title: Build the Base
instructions.text: Heat butter and oil. Add cumin seeds, ginger, garlic, and onion. Sauté until golden brown.

instructions.title: Add the Masala
instructions.text: Add tomato puree, red chili powder, and salt. Cook until oil separates.

instructions.title: Slow Simmer
instructions.text: Add cooked dal and simmer on lowest heat for 45-60 minutes, stirring occasionally. The longer, the better.

instructions.title: Finish with Cream
instructions.text: Add cream, garam masala, butter, and crushed kasuri methi. Serve with naan or rice.

mr.name: दाल मखनी
mr.description: लोण्यात मंद आचेवर शिजवलेली मलईदार काळी डाळ — पंजाबी जेवणाचा अविभाज्य भाग.
mr.chefNotes: कमीत कमी ८ तास मंद आचेवर शिजवा. घाईत बनवू नका.

instructions.title: डाळ भिजवा
instructions.text: काळी डाळ आणि राजमा रात्रभर भिजवा.

instructions.title: शिजवा
instructions.text: कुकरमध्ये ५-६ शिट्या होईपर्यंत शिजवा.

instructions.title: फोडणी द्या
instructions.text: लोण्यात आलं-लसूण पेस्ट, टोमॅटो प्युरी आणि मसाले परतवा.

instructions.title: मंद आचेवर शिजवा
instructions.text: शिजलेली डाळ घालून मंद आचेवर मलईदार होईपर्यंत शिजवा.

instructions.title: सर्व्ह करा
instructions.text: मलई आणि लोणी घालून नान किंवा भातासोबत सर्व्ह करा.

en.name: Paneer Butter Masala
en.description: Soft paneer cubes in a luscious tomato-cashew gravy — India's most beloved vegetarian curry.
en.chefNotes: Never overcook paneer — it becomes rubbery. Add it at the very end and cook on low heat. For silkier gravy, blend the cashew paste extra smooth.

instructions.title: Sauté the Aromatics
instructions.text: Heat butter and oil. Add ginger-garlic paste and sauté until fragrant.

instructions.title: Cook the Gravy Base
instructions.text: Add onion puree and cook until golden. Add tomato puree, chili powder, turmeric, and salt. Cook 10 minutes.

instructions.title: Add Richness
instructions.text: Add cashew paste and cook for 5 minutes for creaminess. Add water and sugar. Simmer.

instructions.title: Add the Paneer
instructions.text: Add paneer cubes gently and cook for 5-7 minutes on low heat.

instructions.title: Finish & Garnish
instructions.text: Add cream, garam masala, and crushed kasuri methi. Serve garnished with cream swirl alongside naan or rice.

mr.name: पनीर बटर मसाला
mr.description: मलईदार टोमॅटो ग्रेव्हीतील मऊ पनीर — शाकाहारी रेस्टॉरंटचा सर्वात लोकप्रिय पदार्थ.
mr.chefNotes: पनीर कधीही जास्त शिजवू नका, रबरासारखे होते. शेवटी हलक्या आचेवर घाला.

instructions.title: पनीर तयार करा
instructions.text: पनीर चौकोनी कापून हलके तळा.

instructions.title: ग्रेव्ही बनवा
instructions.text: लोण्यात टोमॅटो प्युरी, काजू पेस्ट आणि मसाले शिजवा.

instructions.title: मलई घाला
instructions.text: मलई आणि साखर घालून ५ मिनिटे शिजवा.

instructions.title: पनीर घाला
instructions.text: पनीर हळुवारपणे ग्रेव्हीत मिसळा.

instructions.title: सर्व्ह करा
instructions.text: कसुरी मेथी आणि मलई घालून नानसोबत सर्व्ह करा.

en.name: Sarson Ka Saag
en.description: Slow-cooked mustard greens with corn flatbread — a winter ritual that defines Punjab's soul food culture.
en.chefNotes: The corn flour (makki ka atta) is what gives saag its distinctive texture. Don't skip it. True Punjabi saag should be coarsely mashed, never smooth.

instructions.title: Boil the Greens
instructions.text: Wash and roughly chop all greens. Boil with green chilies and ginger for 30-40 minutes until very soft.

instructions.title: Blend Coarsely
instructions.text: Drain most water (save some) and blend coarsely — it should still have texture.

instructions.title: Cook the Base
instructions.text: Heat ghee, add garlic and onion. Cook until golden. Add blended greens, corn flour, and salt.

instructions.title: Slow Simmer
instructions.text: Cook on low heat for 20-25 minutes, stirring frequently. The saag should be thick and rich.

instructions.title: Serve Traditionally
instructions.text: Serve piping hot with a big dollop of white butter, alongside makki di roti and jaggery.

mr.name: सरसों का साग
mr.description: मोहरीच्या पानांची हिवाळी भाजी — मक्याच्या रोटीसोबत पंजाबचे सोल फूड.
mr.chefNotes: मंद आचेवर बराच वेळ शिजवा. लोण्याचा तडका अत्यंत महत्त्वाचा.

instructions.title: भाज्या शिजवा
instructions.text: मोहरीची पाने आणि पालक मऊ होईपर्यंत शिजवा.

instructions.title: ब्लेंड करा
instructions.text: शिजवलेल्या भाज्या जाडसर वाटा.

instructions.title: फोडणी द्या
instructions.text: लोण्यात कांदा, आलं-लसूण परतवा.

instructions.title: एकत्र शिजवा
instructions.text: वाटलेल्या भाज्या आणि मक्याचे पीठ घालून शिजवा.

instructions.title: सर्व्ह करा
instructions.text: लोण्याचा तडका देऊन मक्याच्या रोटीसोबत सर्व्ह करा.

en.name: Amritsari Kulcha
en.description: Flaky stuffed bread from the holy city of Amritsar — crispy outside, molten potato filling inside.
en.chefNotes: The dough must rest for at least 2 hours — this is non-negotiable. The yogurt and baking powder need time to work their magic for that signature flakiness.

instructions.title: Prepare the Dough
instructions.text: Mix flour, yogurt, baking powder, baking soda, oil, salt, and water. Knead soft dough. Rest for 2 hours.

instructions.title: Make the Filling
instructions.text: Mix mashed potatoes, onion, green chilies, cumin seeds, amchur, coriander, and salt.

instructions.title: Stuff & Roll
instructions.text: Flatten dough, place filling in center, seal and roll into thick disc.

instructions.title: Cook on Tawa
instructions.text: Cook on hot tawa with butter on both sides until golden spots appear.

instructions.title: Serve Hot
instructions.text: Serve hot with chole, pickle, and onion rings.

mr.name: अमृतसरी कुलचा
mr.description: बटाट्याचे किंवा पनीरचे सारण भरलेला कुरकुरीत कुलचा — अमृतसरचा स्पेशल.
mr.chefNotes: तंदूरमध्ये भाजल्यास सर्वोत्तम चव येते. लोणी भरपूर लावा.

instructions.title: पीठ मळा
instructions.text: मैदा, दही, मीठ आणि अजवाइन मिसळून मऊ पीठ मळा.

instructions.title: सारण तयार करा
instructions.text: उकडलेले बटाटे मॅश करून मसाले मिसळा.

instructions.title: भरा
instructions.text: पिठाचा गोळा करून सारण भरा आणि लाटा.

instructions.title: भाजा
instructions.text: तव्यावर किंवा तंदूरमध्ये सोनेरी होईपर्यंत भाजा.

instructions.title: सर्व्ह करा
instructions.text: लोणी लावून छोल्यांसोबत सर्व्ह करा.

en.name: Crispy Ghee Dosa
en.description: Paper-thin fermented rice crepes dusted with aromatic gunpowder spice — South India's golden masterpiece.
en.chefNotes: A well-seasoned cast iron tawa is the secret to the perfect dosa. The first dosa is always a sacrifice — it seasons the pan for the rest of the batch.
ingredients: 3 cups rice (idli rice or parboiled rice)

instructions.title: Soak & Grind
instructions.text: Soak rice and urad dal separately for 6 hours. Grind urad dal to smooth batter, rice to slightly coarse paste.

instructions.title: Ferment Overnight
instructions.text: Mix both batters, add salt, and ferment overnight (8-12 hours) until doubled in volume.

instructions.title: Heat the Tawa
instructions.text: Heat a cast iron or non-stick dosa tawa. Sprinkle water and wipe clean.

instructions.title: Spread & Cook
instructions.text: Pour batter in center and spread in circular motion to make a thin crepe. Drizzle ghee around edges.

instructions.title: Serve Crispy
instructions.text: Cook until golden and crispy. For masala dosa, place potato filling and fold. Serve with coconut chutney and sambar.

en.name: Soft Idli
en.description: Cloud-soft steamed rice cakes with a naturally tangy flavor — the ultimate comfort breakfast.
en.chefNotes: The fluffiness comes from properly fermented batter. In cold weather, keep the batter inside an oven with the light on to maintain warmth for fermentation.
ingredients: 2 cups idli rice or parboiled rice

instructions.title: Soak & Grind
instructions.text: Soak rice and urad dal with fenugreek separately for 4-6 hours. Grind urad dal to airy batter, rice to slightly grainy paste.

instructions.title: Ferment
instructions.text: Mix both batters, add salt, and ferment in a warm place for 8-12 hours.

instructions.title: Pour & Steam
instructions.text: Grease idli molds with oil. Pour batter into each mold. Steam for 10-12 minutes.

instructions.title: Unmold & Serve
instructions.text: Let cool for a minute, then unmold using a wet spoon. Serve hot with sambar and coconut chutney.

mr.name: Soft Idli
mr.description: Cloud-soft steamed rice cakes with a naturally tangy flavor — the ultimate comfort breakfast.
mr.chefNotes: The fluffiness comes from properly fermented batter. In cold weather, keep the batter inside an oven with the light on to maintain warmth for fermentation.
ingredients: 2 cups idli rice or parboiled rice

instructions.title: Soak & Grind
instructions.text: Soak rice and urad dal with fenugreek separately for 4-6 hours. Grind urad dal to airy batter, rice to slightly grainy paste.

instructions.title: Ferment
instructions.text: Mix both batters, add salt, and ferment in a warm place for 8-12 hours.

instructions.title: Pour & Steam
instructions.text: Grease idli molds with oil. Pour batter into each mold. Steam for 10-12 minutes.

instructions.title: Unmold & Serve
instructions.text: Let cool for a minute, then unmold using a wet spoon. Serve hot with sambar and coconut chutney.

en.name: Medu Vada
en.description: Crispy, golden doughnut-shaped urad dal fritters — crunchy outside, impossibly fluffy within.
en.chefNotes: The batter must be thick and fluffy — test by dropping a small piece in water; it should float. Never add water while grinding; use ice-cold water only if needed.
ingredients: 1 cup urad dal, soaked for 4 hours

instructions.title: Grind the Dal
instructions.text: Drain soaked urad dal. Grind to a thick, fluffy batter without adding too much water.

instructions.title: Add Seasonings
instructions.text: Add chopped onion, green chilies, ginger, curry leaves, cumin seeds, pepper, coriander, and salt.

instructions.title: Shape the Vadas
instructions.text: Wet your hands. Take batter, shape into a ball, flatten and make a hole in the center.

instructions.title: Deep Fry
instructions.text: Slide gently into hot oil. Fry on medium heat until golden brown and crispy on both sides (4-5 minutes).

instructions.title: Serve Immediately
instructions.text: Drain on paper towels. Serve immediately with coconut chutney and sambar.

mr.name: मेदू वडा
mr.description: कुरकुरीत उडीद डाळीचे वडे — दक्षिण भारतीय नाश्त्याचा अविभाज्य भाग.
mr.chefNotes: पीठ बारीक आणि हवेशीर वाटा. तळताना आच मध्यम ठेवा.

instructions.title: डाळ भिजवा
instructions.text: उडीद डाळ ४ तास भिजवा.

instructions.title: पीठ वाटा
instructions.text: पाणी न घालता बारीक हवेशीर पीठ वाटा.

instructions.title: मसाले मिसळा
instructions.text: कढीपत्ता, मिरची, आलं आणि मीठ मिसळा.

instructions.title: वडे तळा
instructions.text: ओल्या हाताने गोल वडे करून मध्यम आचेवर तळा.

instructions.title: सर्व्ह करा
instructions.text: सांबार आणि नारळ चटणीसोबत सर्व्ह करा.

en.name: Uttapam
en.description: Thick, savory rice pancakes topped with colorful vegetables — India's original pizza.
en.chefNotes: Uttapam is the perfect way to use slightly over-fermented dosa batter — the extra tanginess adds character. Don't flip too early or the toppings will fall off.

instructions.title: Prepare the Batter
instructions.text: Add salt to the fermented dosa batter. The batter should be slightly thicker than dosa batter.

instructions.title: Pour & Spread
instructions.text: Pour batter on greased tawa and spread into a thick round — don't spread too thin.

instructions.title: Add Toppings
instructions.text: Immediately sprinkle onions, tomatoes, green chilies, capsicum, and coriander on top. Press gently.

instructions.title: Cook & Flip
instructions.text: Cover and cook on medium-low heat until base is golden. Flip and cook other side. Serve with chutney and sambar.

mr.name: Uttapam
mr.description: Thick, savory rice pancakes topped with colorful vegetables — India's original pizza.
mr.chefNotes: Uttapam is the perfect way to use slightly over-fermented dosa batter — the extra tanginess adds character. Don't flip too early or the toppings will fall off.

instructions.title: Prepare the Batter
instructions.text: Add salt to the fermented dosa batter. The batter should be slightly thicker than dosa batter.

instructions.title: Pour & Spread
instructions.text: Pour batter on greased tawa and spread into a thick round — don't spread too thin.

instructions.title: Add Toppings
instructions.text: Immediately sprinkle onions, tomatoes, green chilies, capsicum, and coriander on top. Press gently.

instructions.title: Cook & Flip
instructions.text: Cover and cook on medium-low heat until base is golden. Flip and cook other side. Serve with chutney and sambar.

en.name: Sambar
en.description: A tangy-spicy lentil stew brimming with seasonal vegetables — the backbone of South Indian meals.
en.chefNotes: Every South Indian household has their own sambar powder recipe. The tempering at the end is crucial — it's what transforms dal soup into sambar.

instructions.title: Cook the Dal
instructions.text: Pressure cook toor dal with turmeric until soft and mushy. Mash well.

instructions.title: Prepare Tamarind
instructions.text: Soak tamarind in warm water and extract juice. Set aside.

instructions.title: Cook the Vegetables
instructions.text: Cook drumstick, carrot, brinjal, and onions in water until tender. Add tamarind water and sambar powder.

instructions.title: Combine & Temper
instructions.text: Add mashed dal and simmer 10-15 minutes. For tempering: heat oil, add mustard seeds, red chilies, and curry leaves. Pour over sambar.

mr.name: Sambar
mr.description: A tangy-spicy lentil stew brimming with seasonal vegetables — the backbone of South Indian meals.
mr.chefNotes: Every South Indian household has their own sambar powder recipe. The tempering at the end is crucial — it's what transforms dal soup into sambar.

instructions.title: Cook the Dal
instructions.text: Pressure cook toor dal with turmeric until soft and mushy. Mash well.

instructions.title: Prepare Tamarind
instructions.text: Soak tamarind in warm water and extract juice. Set aside.

instructions.title: Cook the Vegetables
instructions.text: Cook drumstick, carrot, brinjal, and onions in water until tender. Add tamarind water and sambar powder.

instructions.title: Combine & Temper
instructions.text: Add mashed dal and simmer 10-15 minutes. For tempering: heat oil, add mustard seeds, red chilies, and curry leaves. Pour over sambar.

en.name: Dhokla
en.description: Spongy steamed gram flour cakes drenched in sweet-spicy tempering — Gujarat's gift to the snacking world.
en.chefNotes: Add the Eno immediately before steaming and pour the batter right away — the reaction begins losing power within seconds. This is what makes or breaks the fluffiness.
ingredients: Fresh coriander and coconut for garnish

instructions.title: Make the Batter
instructions.text: Mix besan, yogurt, water, sugar, ginger-chili paste, turmeric, and salt into a smooth batter.

instructions.title: Steam the Dhokla
instructions.text: Add Eno salt just before steaming and mix gently. Pour into greased plate. Steam for 12-15 minutes.

instructions.title: Prepare Tempering
instructions.text: Heat oil, add mustard seeds, sesame seeds, curry leaves, and slit green chilies.

instructions.title: Assemble & Serve
instructions.text: Add water and sugar to tempering. Pour over cut dhokla pieces. Garnish with coriander and coconut. Serve with green chutney.

mr.name: ढोकळा
mr.description: मऊ, हलका आणि आंबट-गोड वाफवलेला गुजराती नाश्ता.
mr.chefNotes: पीठ नीट आंबवा आणि वाफवताना झाकण उघडू नका.

instructions.title: पीठ तयार करा
instructions.text: बेसन, रवा, दही, पाणी आणि मसाले मिसळून पीठ तयार करा.

instructions.title: इनो मिसळा
instructions.text: इनो घालून लगेच हलवा.

instructions.title: वाफवा
instructions.text: तयार भांड्यात पीठ ओतून २० मिनिटे वाफवा.

instructions.title: फोडणी द्या
instructions.text: मोहरी, मिरची आणि कढीपत्ता तेलात फोडणी देऊन ढोकळ्यावर ओता.

instructions.title: सर्व्ह करा
instructions.text: कापून हिरवी चटणीसोबत सर्व्ह करा.

en.name: Khandvi
en.description: Delicate rolled gram flour sheets with a mustardy tempering — a masterpiece of Gujarati technique.
en.chefNotes: Speed is everything. Once the batter is cooked, you have about 2 minutes before it sets. Have your greased thalis ready. Work in two batches if needed.
ingredients: Fresh coconut and coriander for garnish

instructions.title: Cook the Batter
instructions.text: Whisk besan, buttermilk, water, ginger-chili paste, turmeric, and salt until smooth. Cook on medium heat, stirring continuously for 8-10 minutes.

instructions.title: Test for Readiness
instructions.text: The mixture is ready when it coats the spoon thickly and leaves the sides of the pan.

instructions.title: Spread & Roll
instructions.text: Working quickly, spread thin layers on greased thalis. Let cool 5-7 minutes. Cut into strips and roll tightly.

instructions.title: Temper & Garnish
instructions.text: Heat oil, add mustard seeds, sesame seeds, and curry leaves. Pour over rolls. Garnish with coconut and coriander.

mr.name: खांडवी
mr.description: बेसनाचे पातळ गुंडाळे — गुजरातचा नाजूक आणि चवदार नाश्ता.
mr.chefNotes: पीठ सतत हलवत राहा, गुठळ्या होता कामा नयेत.

instructions.title: पीठ शिजवा
instructions.text: बेसन, दही, हळद, मीठ आणि पाणी मिसळून सतत हलवत शिजवा.

instructions.title: पसरवा
instructions.text: ताटावर पातळ थर पसरवा आणि थंड होऊ द्या.

instructions.title: गुंडाळा
instructions.text: घट्ट होताच पट्ट्या कापून गुंडाळा.

instructions.title: फोडणी द्या
instructions.text: मोहरी, तीळ आणि कढीपत्ता फोडणी द्या.

instructions.title: सर्व्ह करा
instructions.text: नारळ आणि कोथिंबीर घालून सर्व्ह करा.

en.name: Thepla
en.description: Herb-speckled fenugreek flatbreads that stay fresh for days — the quintessential travel companion.
en.chefNotes: Use only fresh fenugreek leaves, not dried. The natural moisture in fresh methi is what keeps theplas soft for days. Add a pinch of sugar to balance the bitterness.

instructions.title: Prepare the Leaves
instructions.text: Wash and finely chop fenugreek leaves. Squeeze out excess water.

instructions.title: Knead the Dough
instructions.text: Mix all ingredients. Knead into soft dough using water as needed. Rest for 15 minutes.

instructions.title: Roll & Cook
instructions.text: Divide into small balls. Roll into thin rotis. Cook on hot tawa with oil/ghee on both sides until golden.

instructions.title: Serve or Pack
instructions.text: Theplas stay fresh for 2-3 days. Serve with pickle, yogurt, or enjoy plain as a snack.

mr.name: थेपला
mr.description: मेथीची चवदार पातळ रोटी — गुजराती प्रवासी नाश्ता.
mr.chefNotes: पीठ मळताना तेल जास्त घाला म्हणजे मऊ होतात.

instructions.title: पीठ मळा
instructions.text: पीठ, मेथी, मसाले, दही आणि तेल मिसळून मऊ पीठ मळा.

instructions.title: विश्रांती द्या
instructions.text: पीठ २०-३० मिनिटे झाकून ठेवा.

instructions.title: लाटा
instructions.text: पातळ गोल लाटा.

instructions.title: भाजा
instructions.text: तव्यावर तेल लावून दोन्ही बाजूंनी भाजा.

instructions.title: सर्व्ह करा
instructions.text: लोणचे आणि दह्यासोबत सर्व्ह करा.

en.name: Handvo
en.description: A hearty savory cake of mixed lentils and bottle gourd — Gujarat's answer to the savory pound cake.
en.chefNotes: The bottom crust should be deeply golden and crispy. If using an oven, bake at 180°C for 30-35 minutes for an even result.

instructions.title: Soak & Grind
instructions.text: Soak rice and all dals together for 4-5 hours. Grind into a slightly coarse batter.

instructions.title: Ferment Overnight
instructions.text: Add yogurt and salt. Ferment the batter overnight.

instructions.title: Add Vegetables
instructions.text: Add grated bottle gourd, ginger-chili paste, and turmeric to the batter.

instructions.title: Cook Slowly
instructions.text: Heat oil with tempering spices. Add Eno to batter, pour into pan. Cover and cook on low heat for 20-25 minutes.

instructions.title: Flip & Serve
instructions.text: Flip and cook other side for 15 minutes. Cut into wedges and serve with green chutney and chai.

mr.name: Handvo

mr.chefNotes: The bottom crust should be deeply golden and crispy. If using an oven, bake at 180°C for 30-35 minutes for an even result.

instructions.title: Ferment Overnight
instructions.text: Add yogurt and salt. Ferment the batter overnight.

instructions.title: Add Vegetables
instructions.text: Add grated bottle gourd, ginger-chili paste, and turmeric to the batter.

instructions.title: Cook Slowly
instructions.text: Heat oil with tempering spices. Add Eno to batter, pour into pan. Cover and cook on low heat for 20-25 minutes.

instructions.title: Flip & Serve
instructions.text: Flip and cook other side for 15 minutes. Cut into wedges and serve with green chutney and chai.

en.name: Rasgulla
en.description: Spongy cottage cheese balls soaked in cardamom-kissed sugar syrup — Bengal's most famous sweet.
en.chefNotes: Knead, knead, knead. If your rasgullas crack in the syrup, you haven't kneaded enough. The chenna must be completely smooth with zero graininess.

instructions.title: Make the Chenna
instructions.text: Boil milk. Add lemon juice gradually while stirring until milk curdles completely. Strain and rinse under cold water.

instructions.title: Knead Until Smooth
instructions.text: Hang for 30 minutes. Knead the chenna on a clean surface for 8-10 minutes until absolutely smooth.

instructions.title: Shape the Balls
instructions.text: Divide into small portions and roll into smooth, crack-free balls.

instructions.title: Cook in Syrup
instructions.text: Prepare sugar syrup. Gently drop chenna balls into boiling syrup. Cover and cook 15 minutes. They will double in size.

instructions.title: Cool & Serve
instructions.text: Add rose water. Refrigerate for 2-3 hours. Serve chilled.

mr.name: Rasgulla
mr.description: Spongy cottage cheese balls soaked in cardamom-kissed sugar syrup — Bengal's most famous sweet.
mr.chefNotes: Knead, knead, knead. If your rasgullas crack in the syrup, you haven't kneaded enough. The chenna must be completely smooth with zero graininess.

instructions.title: Make the Chenna
instructions.text: Boil milk. Add lemon juice gradually while stirring until milk curdles completely. Strain and rinse under cold water.

instructions.title: Knead Until Smooth
instructions.text: Hang for 30 minutes. Knead the chenna on a clean surface for 8-10 minutes until absolutely smooth.

instructions.title: Shape the Balls
instructions.text: Divide into small portions and roll into smooth, crack-free balls.

instructions.title: Cook in Syrup
instructions.text: Prepare sugar syrup. Gently drop chenna balls into boiling syrup. Cover and cook 15 minutes. They will double in size.

instructions.title: Cool & Serve
instructions.text: Add rose water. Refrigerate for 2-3 hours. Serve chilled.

en.name: Sandesh
en.description: Delicate molded milk fudge scented with cardamom and saffron — Bengal's most elegant confection.
en.chefNotes: The quality of milk is paramount. Use the freshest, creamiest full-fat milk available. Sandesh is a celebration of pure dairy — there's nowhere for mediocre milk to hide.

instructions.title: Make Fresh Chenna
instructions.text: Boil milk and curdle with lemon juice. Strain and rinse chenna. Squeeze out water thoroughly.

instructions.title: Knead Smooth
instructions.text: Knead the chenna for 5-7 minutes until very smooth.

instructions.title: Cook Gently
instructions.text: Cook chenna in non-stick pan on low heat with sugar and cardamom. Stir continuously for 5-7 minutes.

instructions.title: Shape & Garnish
instructions.text: Shape into rounds or use a mold while still warm. Garnish with saffron and pistachios. Refrigerate and serve chilled.

mr.name: संदेश
mr.description: छेन्यापासून बनवलेली बंगाली मिठाई — हलकी, गोड आणि सुगंधी.
mr.chefNotes: छेना अतिशय ताजे असावे. जास्त मळल्यास मऊपणा येतो.

instructions.title: छेना बनवा
instructions.text: दूध उकळवून लिंबाचा रस घालून फाडा. कपड्यात गाळा.

instructions.title: मळा
instructions.text: छेना गुळगुळीत होईपर्यंत मळा.

instructions.title: गोड करा
instructions.text: साखर पूड आणि वेलची पूड मिसळा.

instructions.title: आकार द्या
instructions.text: साच्यात दाबून आकार द्या.

instructions.title: सजवा
instructions.text: केशर आणि पिस्ता लावून थंड करून सर्व्ह करा.

en.name: Macher Jhol
en.description: A light, aromatic fish curry cooked in mustard oil — the everyday comfort of a Bengali home.
en.chefNotes: Mustard oil is non-negotiable for authentic flavor. Heat it until it smokes slightly, then reduce — this removes the raw pungency and unlocks the nutty sweetness.
ingredients: 500g rohu or katla fish, cut into pieces

instructions.title: Marinate the Fish
instructions.text: Marinate fish with turmeric and salt for 15 minutes.

instructions.title: Fry the Fish
instructions.text: Heat mustard oil, fry fish until golden on both sides. Set aside.

instructions.title: Build the Curry
instructions.text: Add nigella seeds, onions, ginger paste, and spices. Fry potatoes, add tomatoes.

instructions.title: Simmer Together
instructions.text: Add warm water, salt, and green chilies. Simmer until potatoes are cooked. Gently add fried fish. Simmer 5-7 minutes.

instructions.title: Serve with Rice
instructions.text: Garnish with fresh coriander. Serve hot with steamed rice.

mr.name: माछेर झोल
mr.description: बंगाली पारंपारिक मासे करी — सोपी, हलकी आणि सुगंधी.
mr.chefNotes: मासे आधी हलके तळा. ग्रेव्हीत जास्त शिजवू नका.

instructions.title: मासे तळा
instructions.text: मासे हळद आणि मीठ लावून हलके तळा.

instructions.title: मसाला परतवा
instructions.text: मोहरीच्या तेलात जिरे, कांदा परतवा.

instructions.title: बटाटे शिजवा
instructions.text: बटाटे आणि टोमॅटो घालून शिजवा.

instructions.title: करी बनवा
instructions.text: पाणी, हळद आणि मीठ घालून उकळवा.

instructions.title: सर्व्ह करा
instructions.text: मासे करीत ठेवून भातासोबत सर्व्ह करा.

en.name: Mishti Doi
en.description: Caramelized sweet yogurt set in earthen pots — a Bengali celebration in every spoonful.
en.chefNotes: The earthen pot (handi) is not just aesthetic — it absorbs excess moisture and adds a subtle earthy flavor. If using glass bowls, the setting time may be slightly longer.
ingredients: 1/2 cup sugar (or jaggery for nolen gurer version)

instructions.title: Reduce the Milk
instructions.text: Pour milk into heavy-bottomed pan. Boil and reduce to about 3/4 of original quantity.

instructions.title: Caramelize Sugar
instructions.text: In a separate pan, caramelize sugar until golden amber. Carefully add to hot milk. Stir to dissolve.

instructions.title: Add Culture
instructions.text: Let milk cool to lukewarm. Whisk yogurt starter and add to milk. Mix gently.

instructions.title: Set & Chill
instructions.text: Pour into earthen pots or glass bowls. Keep warm for 6-8 hours to set. Refrigerate once set. Garnish with nuts.

en.name: Fry Modak
en.description: A crispy and golden Maharashtrian festival sweet filled with coconut, jaggery, and aromatic cardamom, traditionally prepared during Ganesh Chaturthi.
en.chefNotes: For extra crispy modaks, add a little semolina to the dough and fry them on a medium-low flame. Never fry on high heat, as the outer layer may brown quickly while remaining uncooked inside. Adding a teaspoon of roasted poppy seeds to the filling gives an authentic festive flavor.
ingredients: Oil or ghee for deep frying

instructions.title: Prepare the Dough
instructions.text: Mix flour, semolina, salt, and ghee. Gradually add water and knead into a smooth, firm dough. Cover and let it rest for 20 minutes.

instructions.title: Make the Filling
instructions.text: Heat a pan and combine grated coconut and jaggery. Cook until the jaggery melts and blends well. Add cardamom powder, chopped nuts, raisins, and poppy seeds. Allow the mixture to cool completely.

instructions.title: Shape the Modaks
instructions.text: Divide the dough into small balls and roll each into a thin circle. Place a spoonful of filling in the center, create pleats around the edges, and seal tightly to form the classic modak shape.

instructions.title: Deep Fry
instructions.text: Heat oil or ghee on medium-low flame. Fry the modaks in batches until they become evenly golden brown and crisp. Drain on paper towels.

instructions.title: Serve
instructions.text: Allow the fried modaks to cool slightly before serving. They taste delicious warm and can also be stored in an airtight container for several days.

en.name: Moong Dal
en.description: A wholesome and comforting yellow moong dal cooked with aromatic spices, tomatoes, and a flavorful tempering. Perfect with steamed rice or roti.
en.chefNotes: Roasting the moong dal lightly before cooking gives it a rich, nutty aroma. Finish with a spoonful of ghee just before serving for authentic homemade flavor.

instructions.title: Cook the Dal
instructions.text: Wash the moong dal thoroughly. Pressure cook it with water, turmeric, and salt for 2-3 whistles until soft.

instructions.title: Prepare the Tempering
instructions.text: Heat ghee or oil in a pan. Add cumin seeds and mustard seeds. Once they splutter, add chopped onions and sauté until golden.

instructions.title: Cook the Masala
instructions.text: Add ginger-garlic paste and green chilies. Stir for a minute, then add tomatoes, coriander powder, and red chili powder. Cook until the tomatoes become soft.

instructions.title: Combine Everything
instructions.text: Pour the cooked dal into the pan and mix well. Add garam masala and simmer for 5-7 minutes. Adjust the consistency by adding water if needed.

instructions.title: Serve
instructions.text: Garnish with fresh coriander leaves and a squeeze of lemon juice. Serve hot with steamed rice, jeera rice, roti, or chapati.

en.name: Chawli Chi Bhaji
en.description: A simple and nutritious Maharashtrian stir-fry made with fresh chawli (cowpea) leaves, lightly seasoned with garlic, green chilies, and traditional spices.
en.chefNotes: Avoid adding water while cooking, as the leaves release enough moisture. Fresh grated coconut enhances the authentic Maharashtrian flavor, and garlic gives the bhaji its signature aroma.
ingredients: 4 cups fresh chawli (cowpea) leaves, washed and chopped

instructions.title: Prepare the Leaves
instructions.text: Wash the chawli leaves thoroughly, remove thick stems, and chop them finely.

instructions.title: Make the Tempering
instructions.text: Heat oil in a pan. Add mustard seeds and cumin seeds. Once they crackle, add crushed garlic and green chilies. Sauté until fragrant.

instructions.title: Cook the Onion
instructions.text: Add the chopped onion and cook until it turns soft and light golden.

instructions.title: Cook the Bhaji
instructions.text: Add turmeric, red chili powder, and chopped chawli leaves. Mix well, cover, and cook on low flame for 10–12 minutes, stirring occasionally. Add salt and cook until the leaves become tender.

instructions.title: Finish and Serve
instructions.text: Sprinkle grated fresh coconut, mix gently, and serve hot with jowar bhakri, bajra bhakri, chapati, or steamed rice and dal.

en.name: Lal Shev Bhaji
en.description: A spicy Maharashtrian-style red curry made with crispy gram flour sev simmered in a rich onion-tomato gravy. This comforting rassedar bhaji pairs perfectly with bhakri, chapati, or steamed rice.
en.chefNotes: Always add the sev at the end of cooking. If added too early, it will become overly soft and lose its texture. For authentic Maharashtrian flavor, use Kolhapuri red chili powder and a little goda masala.

instructions.title: Prepare the Tempering
instructions.text: Heat oil in a pan. Add mustard seeds, cumin seeds, and curry leaves. Once they splutter, add chopped onions and sauté until golden brown.

instructions.title: Cook the Masala
instructions.text: Add ginger-garlic paste and green chilies. Sauté for a minute, then add tomato puree, turmeric, red chili powder, coriander powder, cumin powder, goda masala, garam masala, and salt. Cook until the oil separates.

instructions.title: Make the Rassa
instructions.text: Pour in hot water and bring the curry to a boil. Simmer for 8-10 minutes so the spices blend well and the gravy thickens slightly.

instructions.title: Add the Sev
instructions.text: Just before serving, add the red sev to the boiling gravy and cook for 2-3 minutes. The sev should soften slightly while still retaining some texture.

instructions.title: Serve Hot
instructions.text: Garnish with fresh coriander leaves and serve immediately with jowar bhakri, chapati, pav, or steamed rice.

en.name: Sukki Shev Bhaji
en.description: A traditional Maharashtrian dry-style Sev Bhaji made with spicy onion masala and thick gram flour sev. This flavorful dish is commonly served with jowar bhakri, chapati, or bajra bhakri.
en.chefNotes: Use thick Maharashtrian sev for the best texture. Add only a small amount of water while mixing so the sev remains soft yet doesn't become mushy. A pinch of jaggery can be added to balance the spice if desired.

instructions.title: Prepare the Tempering
instructions.text: Heat oil in a kadai. Add mustard seeds, cumin seeds, and curry leaves. Once they crackle, add chopped green chilies and onions. Sauté until the onions turn golden brown.

instructions.title: Cook the Masala
instructions.text: Add ginger-garlic paste and cook for a minute. Mix in turmeric, red chili powder, coriander powder, cumin powder, goda masala, garam masala, and salt. Sprinkle a few tablespoons of water and cook the masala until aromatic.

instructions.title: Add the Sev
instructions.text: Add the thick sev to the masala and gently mix so that every piece is coated with the spices. Sprinkle 2-3 tablespoons of water to soften the sev slightly without making it soggy.

instructions.title: Steam Briefly
instructions.text: Cover the pan and cook on low flame for 2-3 minutes until the sev absorbs the flavors and softens slightly.

instructions.title: Garnish and Serve
instructions.text: Turn off the heat and garnish with fresh coriander and grated coconut. Serve hot with jowar bhakri, bajra bhakri, chapati, or plain curd.

en.name: Kurdai Chi Bhaji
en.description: A traditional Maharashtrian curry made with crispy fried Kurdai (sun-dried wheat noodles) cooked in a flavorful onion-tomato gravy. This nostalgic village-style dish is best enjoyed with chapati, bhakri, or steamed rice.
en.chefNotes: Always fry the Kurdai before adding them to the curry. Add them only at the end of cooking, as they soften very quickly. For an authentic Maharashtrian taste, use homemade Goda Masala and serve the bhaji hot.
ingredients: take 5 to 10 kurdai and soak in warm/cold water for 5 to 10 minutes

instructions.title: Soak the Kurdai in water for 5 to 10 minutes
instructions.text: Soak the Kurdai in warm/cold water for 5 to 10 minutes until they soften. Drain and set aside.

instructions.title: Prepare the Masala
instructions.text: Heat oil in a pan. Add mustard seeds, cumin seeds, and curry leaves. Once they splutter, add onions and sauté until golden. Add ginger-garlic paste and green chilies, then cook for a minute.

instructions.title: Cook the Gravy
instructions.text: Add tomatoes, turmeric, red chili powder, coriander powder, goda masala, garam masala, and salt. Cook until the tomatoes soften and oil separates.

instructions.title: Add Water and Kurdai
instructions.text: Pour in hot water and bring the gravy to a gentle boil. Add the softened Kurdai and simmer for 2–3 minutes until they absorb the flavorful gravy while still retaining some texture.

instructions.title: Garnish and Serve
instructions.text: Garnish with freshly chopped coriander leaves and serve immediately with chapati, bhakri, or steamed rice.

en.name: Chicken Lollipop
en.description: A popular Indo-Chinese appetizer featuring frenched chicken wings marinated with aromatic spices, coated in a flavorful batter, and deep-fried until crispy and golden. Perfect with Schezwan sauce or spicy mayo.
en.chefNotes: For restaurant-style crispy chicken lollipops, marinate the chicken for at least 2 hours or overnight. Rice flour makes the coating extra crunchy, and double-frying for 1–2 minutes before serving gives an even crispier texture.

instructions.title: Marinate the Chicken
instructions.text: Wash and pat dry the chicken lollipops. Mix ginger-garlic paste, soy sauce, chili sauce, vinegar, pepper, chili powder, garam masala, and salt. Coat the chicken well and marinate for at least 30 minutes.

instructions.title: Prepare the Batter
instructions.text: Add the egg, cornflour, maida, and rice flour to the marinated chicken. Mix thoroughly until every piece is evenly coated with a thick batter.

instructions.title: Deep Fry
instructions.text: Heat oil to medium-high temperature (170–175°C). Fry the chicken lollipops in batches for 6–8 minutes until crispy, golden brown, and cooked through.

instructions.title: Drain and Rest
instructions.text: Remove the fried lollipops and place them on a wire rack or paper towels to remove excess oil while keeping them crispy.

instructions.title: Garnish and Serve
instructions.text: Garnish with chopped spring onion greens and serve hot with Schezwan sauce, garlic dip, or spicy mayonnaise.

en.name: Chicken Tikka
en.description: Juicy boneless chicken cubes marinated in spiced yogurt and grilled to perfection, delivering a smoky, flavorful, and tender appetizer loved across India.
en.chefNotes: Hung curd gives the best coating and prevents the marinade from becoming watery. Marinating overnight results in exceptionally juicy and flavorful chicken. For authentic restaurant-style smoky flavor, place a small bowl with hot charcoal inside the cooked chicken, drizzle a little ghee on the charcoal, cover for 3–5 minutes, and then serve.
ingredients: 500g boneless chicken (thigh or breast), cut into cubes

instructions.title: Prepare the Marinade
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine hung curd, ginger-garlic paste, lemon juice, mustard oil, Kashmiri red chili powder, red chili powder, turmeric, coriander powder, cumin powder, garam masala, chaat masala, crushed kasuri methi, roasted besan, and salt.

instructions.title: Marinate the Chicken
instructions.text: Add the chicken cubes to the marinade and coat them evenly. Cover and refrigerate for at least 2 hours or overnight for the best flavor and tenderness.

instructions.title: Skewer the Chicken
instructions.text: Thread the marinated chicken onto skewers, alternating with onion and capsicum cubes if desired.

instructions.title: Grill the Tikka
instructions.text: Grill in a preheated oven at 220°C for 18–20 minutes, an air fryer at 200°C for 15–18 minutes, or on a charcoal grill until the chicken is cooked through and lightly charred. Baste with butter halfway through cooking.

instructions.title: Serve Hot
instructions.text: Sprinkle chaat masala over the grilled chicken tikka and serve with mint chutney, onion rings, and lemon wedges.

mr.name: Chicken Tikka
mr.description: Juicy boneless chicken cubes marinated in spiced yogurt and grilled to perfection, delivering a smoky, flavorful, and tender appetizer loved across India.
mr.chefNotes: Hung curd gives the best coating and prevents the marinade from becoming watery. Marinating overnight results in exceptionally juicy and flavorful chicken. For authentic restaurant-style smoky flavor, place a small bowl with hot charcoal inside the cooked chicken, drizzle a little ghee on the charcoal, cover for 3–5 minutes, and then serve.
ingredients: 500g boneless chicken (thigh or breast), cut into cubes

instructions.title: Prepare the Marinade
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine hung curd, ginger-garlic paste, lemon juice, mustard oil, Kashmiri red chili powder, red chili powder, turmeric, coriander powder, cumin powder, garam masala, chaat masala, crushed kasuri methi, roasted besan, and salt.

instructions.title: Marinate the Chicken
instructions.text: Add the chicken cubes to the marinade and coat them evenly. Cover and refrigerate for at least 2 hours or overnight for the best flavor and tenderness.

instructions.title: Skewer the Chicken
instructions.text: Thread the marinated chicken onto skewers, alternating with onion and capsicum cubes if desired.

instructions.title: Grill the Tikka
instructions.text: Grill in a preheated oven at 220°C for 18–20 minutes, an air fryer at 200°C for 15–18 minutes, or on a charcoal grill until the chicken is cooked through and lightly charred. Baste with butter halfway through cooking.

instructions.title: Serve Hot
instructions.text: Sprinkle chaat masala over the grilled chicken tikka and serve with mint chutney, onion rings, and lemon wedges.

en.name: Methi Chi Bhaji
en.description: A healthy Maharashtrian dry vegetable dish made with fresh fenugreek leaves, onions, garlic, and mild spices. It pairs wonderfully with bhakri, chapati, or dal-rice.
en.chefNotes: Fresh methi leaves naturally have a slight bitterness. Avoid overcooking them, and add freshly grated coconut at the end for an authentic Maharashtrian flavor.
ingredients: 4 cups fresh fenugreek (Methi) leaves, cleaned and chopped

instructions.title: Prepare the Methi
instructions.text: Separate the methi leaves from the stems, wash thoroughly 2–3 times, drain the excess water, and chop them finely.

instructions.title: Prepare the Tempering
instructions.text: Heat oil in a kadai. Add mustard seeds and cumin seeds. Once they crackle, add crushed garlic and green chilies. Sauté until fragrant.

instructions.title: Cook the Onion
instructions.text: Add the chopped onion and cook until soft and light golden in color.

instructions.title: Cook the Bhaji
instructions.text: Add turmeric, red chili powder, and chopped methi leaves. Mix well, add salt, cover, and cook on low flame for 8–10 minutes, stirring occasionally.

instructions.title: Finish and Serve
instructions.text: Add grated coconut and roasted peanut powder, mix gently, and serve hot with jowar bhakri, chapati, or dal-rice.

mr.name: मेथीची भाजी
mr.description: ताजी मेथी, कांदा, लसूण आणि हलक्या मसाल्यांपासून बनवलेली पौष्टिक महाराष्ट्रीयन सुकी भाजी. भाकरी, चपाती किंवा वरण-भातासोबत खूप छान लागते.
mr.chefNotes: ताज्या मेथीला किंचित कडूपणा असतो. भाजी जास्त शिजवू नका आणि शेवटी ताजा किसलेला नारळ घातल्यास अस्सल महाराष्ट्रीयन चव मिळते.
ingredients: ४ कप ताजी मेथीची पाने, स्वच्छ धुऊन चिरलेली

instructions.title: मेथी तयार करा
instructions.text: मेथीची पाने देठापासून वेगळी करून २–३ वेळा स्वच्छ धुवा, पाणी निथळू द्या आणि बारीक चिरा.

instructions.title: फोडणी द्या
instructions.text: कढईत तेल गरम करून मोहरी व जिरे तडतडू द्या. त्यात ठेचलेला लसूण आणि हिरव्या मिरच्या घालून परता.

instructions.title: कांदा परता
instructions.text: बारीक चिरलेला कांदा घालून तो मऊ आणि हलका सोनेरी होईपर्यंत परता.

instructions.title: भाजी शिजवा
instructions.text: हळद, लाल तिखट आणि चिरलेली मेथी घालून चांगले मिसळा. मीठ घालून झाकण ठेवून मंद आचेवर ८–१० मिनिटे शिजवा.

instructions.title: सर्व्ह करा
instructions.text: शेवटी किसलेला नारळ आणि शेंगदाण्याचा कूट घालून हलकेच मिसळा. गरमागरम ज्वारीची भाकरी, चपाती किंवा वरण-भातासोबत सर्व्ह करा.
